claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 16 years old and lives with me. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have a 16-year-old son named Alex who lives with you.

Since Alex is 16, there are specific rules that apply. You can normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20, but they must stay in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he studying at school, college, or doing an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, he's just finished school recently. He's registered with the careers service to get some advice about what to do next - whether to go to college or look for work. He's in that in-between stage right now, you know?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for explaining that. That's helpful to know that Alex has registered with the careers service after finishing school.

According to the rules, you can apply for Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with either a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. So this is relevant to your situation.

To make sure I have a complete picture, I need to ask: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Just to make sure I have all the information I need: **Are you the person responsible for Alex - meaning you live with him and/or you're paying towards his upkeep (such as food, clothes, pocket money)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. He lives with me and I'm responsible for him - I pay for everything he needs, his food, clothes, all of that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

One final question to complete the assessment: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: Since Alex is 16 and has recently finished school but has registered with a government-sponsored careers service, you can claim Child Benefit for up to **20 weeks** while he's registered with the careers service and deciding what to do next.

You meet all the eligibility requirements:
- You live in the UK
- Alex lives with you
- You're responsible for him and paying for his upkeep
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

**Important to note:** This 20-week period applies while Alex is registered with the careers service. If during this time he:
- Starts approved education or training (like college), you can continue to claim Child Benefit until he turns 20
- Gets a job working 24 hours or more per week, your Child Benefit would stop
- Starts an apprenticeship or claims certain benefits in his own right, your Child Benefit would stop

I hope this helps clarify your eligibility! If you haven't already claimed, you can proceed with your Child Benefit application.

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old and has just finished school. He has registered with the careers service. According to the Child Benefit rules, you can apply for Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with either a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), Alex lives with them, the claimant is responsible for Alex and pays for all his needs (food, clothes, etc.), and nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex for up to 20 weeks while he is registered with the careers service.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 16-year-old son Alex. Alex has recently left education and registered with the careers service, which allows the claimant to claim Child Benefit for up to 20 weeks. The claimant meets all the basic eligibility criteria: they live in the UK, are responsible for Alex, Alex lives with them, and no one else is claiming for him.

Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex for up to 20 weeks while he is registered with the careers service.
